title: Department of War Awards Potential $847M OTAs to Advance Joint Laser Weapon System Development
authors_or_org: ExecutiveGov, reporting Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering (U.S. Department of War)
claim_supported: $86M combined initial OTA awards to nLIGHT Defense and Lockheed Martin Aculight under JLWS; $847M program ceiling; 150kW prototypes scaling to 300-500kW.
locator: Article body, contract-value and power-roadmap paragraphs, dated 2026-07-10
notes: Independent outlet confirming/refreshing atlas L12-031 (defensescoop, same event) with the explicit $847M ceiling figure directly quoted.

india_origin_audit.notes: US government-contracting trade press (Washington, DC based GovExec/ExecutiveGov); reports a US federal government contract action.

title: Phase Change Material Solutions for Directed Energy Applications
claim_supported: ACT sells custom-engineered PCM heat sinks/exchangers explicitly for 30+kW directed-energy systems; ITAR-registered, AS9100/ISO9001-certified, HQ Lancaster PA.
locator: Product page body, capabilities and directed-energy application paragraphs
notes: Contradicts the seed's claim of 'no productized burst-mode thermal storage LRU identified' -- ACT is a going-concern merchant competitor in this exact niche at the 30kW+ tier.

india_origin_audit.notes: US company, HQ Lancaster, PA.

title: General Atomics Tests Thermal Energy Storage Device for Directed Energy Weapons
claim_supported: GA Advanced Power Systems Division tested a 3MJ, 35kg wax-PCM thermal storage module rated at 230kW average heat-storage rate, built for directed-energy weapon cooling (laser and HPM).
locator: Press release body, device-scale and program-context paragraphs, dated 2010-06-02
notes: Historical (2010) but establishes GA as a prime with long-standing in-house DEW thermal-storage capability -- a second incumbent alongside ACT, further correcting the seed's 'no productized LRU' claim.

india_origin_audit.notes: US company, HQ San Diego, CA.

title: The US Army is already ditching its most powerful laser weapon yet
claim_supported: Army divesting IFPC-HEL 'Valkyrie' (300kW) as a fielding candidate, folding lessons into JLWS; DE M-SHORAD found 'not mature enough'; HELSI pursuing megawatt-class lasers.
locator: Article body, program-status paragraphs, dated 2026-03-23
notes: Shows DEW investment consolidating around JLWS/HELSI (megawatt-class) rather than fragmenting -- reinforces, not undermines, the megawatt burst-thermal-load demand thesis.

india_origin_audit.notes: US defense trade press, HQ Vienna, VA.

title: Low-Cost Composite Phase Change Material
authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy, Consortium for Materials for Extreme-Environment Innovation (CMEI)
claim_supported: Paraffin PCM ~$20-40/kWh-thermal; salt-hydrate PCM <$2/kWh-thermal (raw material, building-scale); ORNL-partnered DOE project targeting 10x PCM cost reduction.
locator: Article body, cost-figures paragraph, dated 2019-03-05
notes: Building-scale commodity PCM cost, not aerospace/defense-grade -- used only as a price-signal floor/anchor for the bottom-up arithmetic, with markup explicitly labeled as an assumption.

india_origin_audit.notes: US federal government agency.

title: The US military wants to showcase battle-ready laser weapons by 2028
claim_supported: Army's Enduring High Energy Laser (E-HEL): 30kW modular counter-drone system, 24 units planned over five years, first prototype Q2 FY2026, initial procurement units by end FY2027; billed as the Army's first true DE program of record.
locator: Article body, program-timeline paragraphs, dated 2026-06-02
notes: Second, independent (different program) government DEW procurement signal; lower power tier (30kW) than JLWS, so only indirectly relevant to the MW-scale thermal-magazine thesis.

india_origin_audit.notes: US defense trade press.

title: White House Launches Space Nuclear Initiative, Sets Timeline for Lunar Reactors
authors_or_org: POWER Magazine, reporting White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (NSTM-3)
claim_supported: NSTM-3 (issued 2026-04-14) directs NASA to develop a >=100kWe reactor for the 2030s and a mid-power (>=20kWe) lunar variant for 2030, with downselect to <=2 designs within one year.
locator: Article body, directive-timeline paragraphs, published 2026-04-16
notes: Primary/official trigger source corroborating atlas L09-033's 100kWe/2030s claim independently.

institutions.name: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy

india_origin_audit.notes: US energy-industry trade press reporting a US federal policy directive.

title: Great News for NASA in the House-Senate FY2026 Appropriations Report
claim_supported: $24.438B total NASA FY2026 appropriation including $250M specifically for Fission Surface Power to support a 2030 lunar reactor; Congress rejected a ~24% proposed NASA cut.
locator: Article body, appropriations-figures paragraph, published/updated 2026-01-05/01-10
notes: Independent second demand source: confirms funded, not just directed, program status.

india_origin_audit.notes: US space-policy trade press/institute, reporting US congressional appropriations.

title: NASA Picks Three Nuclear Power Concepts for Demonstration on the Moon
authors_or_org: POWER Magazine, reporting NASA/DOE/Battelle Energy Alliance
claim_supported: Three FSP Phase-1 teams confirmed: (1) Lockheed Martin+BWXT+Creare, (2) Westinghouse+Aerojet Rocketdyne, (3) IX[Intuitive Machines/X-Energy]+Maxar+Boeing; $5M/team 12-month contracts for a 40kWe system, published 2022-06-23.
locator: Article body, team-composition and contract-value paragraphs
notes: Historical baseline confirming the seed's team-composition claims and the 40kWe->100kWe re-baseline; not counted toward the 2030-timing requirement (dated 2022).

india_origin_audit.notes: US energy trade press reporting a NASA/DOE program action.

title: UK moon base dreams stall as Rolls-Royce halts nuclear reactor work
claim_supported: Rolls-Royce's UK Space Agency-funded (GBP9M) 100kWe lunar micro-reactor program is paused after funding expired/no launch partner; NASA/DOE restrict FSP bidding to domestic American firms.
locator: Article body, funding-status and bidding-restriction paragraphs, dated 2026-02-02
notes: Confirms FSP is structurally a US-domestic-only competition (favorable context for a US merchant entrant) and provides global (non-market) competitor context.

india_origin_audit.notes: US-based technology trade press; Rolls-Royce HQ London, UK.

title: About Brayton - Brayton Energy
claim_supported: Brayton Energy LLC (Hampton, NH) is an independent merchant turbomachinery R&D house serving 80+ clients across government and industry programs, with founder heritage in solar-dynamic space power (Lockheed/Sanders).
locator: About page body, company-description and client-base paragraphs
notes: Material correction to the seed's 'merchant multi-team supplier position is open' framing -- an independent merchant Brayton-turbomachinery supplier already exists, though not confirmed on FSP specifically.

india_origin_audit.notes: US company, HQ Hampton, NH.

title: Brayton Cycle Power Generation in Space
claim_supported: Creare (Hanover, NH) has ~40 years of closed-Brayton space-power heritage; currently developing a NASA-funded small-scale converter targeting 26% efficiency at 350W/164,000rpm.
locator: Page body, heritage and current-project paragraphs
notes: Confirms the seed's claim that Creare is inside the Lockheed FSP team with real, current (not only historical) Brayton conversion work.

india_origin_audit.notes: US company, HQ Hanover, NH.

title: Affordable Ka-Band Metamaterial-Based Electronically Scanned Array Radar for Test and Training (Topic A254-049)
claim_supported: Topic A254-049, Solicitation 25.4, open 2026-04-15 to 2026-05-13, Phase I up to $250,000, target production cost $300,000/unit, 30-40GHz, beam-switching 10-20us, 20km range. No Phase I award announced as of this session.
locator: Topic page body, dates/funding/technical-target fields
notes: Primary government solicitation; refreshes/confirms atlas L16-020. As of 2026-07-13 this remains a single, unawarded Phase I opportunity -- the seed's own 2028 second-anchor gate is not yet satisfied.

india_origin_audit.notes: US federal government program.

title: Metamaterial Based Antenna (Topic A214-029)
claim_supported: Closed 2021 Army Aviation SBIR topic for metamaterial-based Ku/K/Ka-band SATCOM antennas (AH-64E/CH-47F/UH-60M/FVL) -- a different program office and application (communications antennas, not beam-steering radar) than A254-049.
locator: Topic page body, scope and dates fields
notes: Tested as a candidate 'second demand anchor' for the seed's 2028 gate; does NOT satisfy the gate (different program, different application, historical/closed) but shows recurring Army institutional interest in metamaterial RF technology.

india_origin_audit.notes: US federal government program.

title: Echodyne Opens New Manufacturing Facility to Meet Surging Global Demand for Advanced MESA Radar
claim_supported: Echodyne opened an $40M, 86,350sqft Washington-state facility with 30,000+ radar/year capacity, driven by C-UAS/force-protection/BVLOS demand; integrated into Anduril/Axon/Moog/Northrop Grumman products.
locator: Article body, facility-scale and market-driver paragraphs, dated 2026-07
notes: Material correction to the seed's framing of Echodyne as a narrower 'K/Ku-band incumbent' -- shows a much larger-scale, well-capitalized competitor.

india_origin_audit.notes: Echodyne HQ Kirkland, WA.

title: Echodyne Named as Radar System for Trust Automation's $490M Air Force Counter-UAS Engineering Contract
authors_or_org: UAS Weekly, reporting Echodyne Corp. / Trust Automation
claim_supported: Echodyne's EchoShield radar selected as primary radar for Trust Automation's SUADS C-UAS platform family under a $490M USAF IDIQ contract.
locator: Article body, contract-value and role paragraphs, dated 2026-04-23
notes: Demand/competitor context confirming the broader counter-UAS radar market is scaling now via a well-capitalized adjacent competitor.

india_origin_audit.notes: US trade press; Echodyne HQ Kirkland, WA.

title: 光启技术再签5.16亿元超材料订单 (Guangqi Technology signs additional RMB516M metamaterial order)
claim_supported: Guangqi Technology (SZSE:002625) signed RMB1.278 billion in metamaterial batch-production contracts with five customers (metamaterial aerospace structural components), for delivery by 2026-06-30.
locator: Article body: '近期累计与五家客户签订了合计12.78亿元的超材料产品批产合同' paragraph, dated 2025-09-08
notes: Corroborates the seed's 'multi-billion-RMB recurring category' claim at the company-revenue level (atlas L16-017); products described are aerospace structural/likely stealth components, not confirmed as Ka-band beam-steering ESA tiles specifically.

institutions.name: Guangqi Technology Co., Ltd. (光启技术)

india_origin_audit.notes: China-based financial trade press; Guangqi listed on Shenzhen Stock Exchange (002625.SZ).

title: Breaking New Ground in Stealth Technology: KRISS Develops Core Radar Components Domestically
authors_or_org: Korea Research Institute of Standards and Science (KRISS)
claim_supported: KRISS developed Frequency-Selective-Surface (FSS) radome design software and an electromagnetic-wave stealth-evaluation system (radar cross-section reduction), transferred to Korea Electrotechnology Research (KER) for KRW500M; underlying research published IEEE TMTT 2025-07-18.
locator: Release body, technology-description and transfer-value paragraphs
notes: Material correction to atlas L16-018's framing: this is passive-stealth/RCS-reduction metamaterial technology, not active beam-steering ESA technology -- confirms the seed's own 'upside only, excluded from base case' caution was appropriate.

institutions.name: Korea Research Institute of Standards and Science (KRISS)

india_origin_audit.notes: South Korean government research institute.

title: Bergen Engines secures 400MW Contract for US AI Data Center Power Plant
claim_supported: Named 400MW US East Coast off-grid AI data-center power plant (buyer/EPC: E-Finity Distributed Generation), 36x11.2MW Bergen engines, deploying Piller SHIELDX flywheel-based Dynamic Power Stabilization to absorb sub-second AI load-step transients up to 30%.
locator: Press release body, buyer/scope/technology paragraphs, dated 2025-11-26
notes: Refreshes/extends atlas L16-052 with the named buyer/EPC (E-Finity) and the explicit 30%-load-step-transient figure; strongest single demand datapoint in this assignment.

india_origin_audit.notes: Bergen Engines HQ Norway, owned by UK's Langley Holdings; named buyer/EPC E-Finity Distributed Generation is US-based.

title: GE Vernova Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
claim_supported: GE Vernova's Electrification segment booked $2.4B in Q1-2026 equipment orders to support data centers alone -- more than all of 2025 combined, per CEO Scott Strazik.
locator: Press release body, Electrification-segment results paragraph, dated 2026-04-22
notes: Independent second demand source (different company/product category than Piller) confirming the broader datacenter-electrification buildout wave is real and accelerating.

india_origin_audit.notes: US public company (NYSE: GEV), HQ Cambridge, MA.

title: About Us - Stornetic
claim_supported: Stornetic GmbH (Julich, Germany; subsidiary of Enrichment Technology Limited) makes composite carbon-fiber flywheels (EnWheel) derived from 40 years of uranium-centrifuge manufacturing heritage; installations at EDF (France), KIT (Germany), Nippon Koei (Japan); no datacenter-specific deployment found.
locator: About-us page body, technology and installation-reference paragraphs
notes: Close architectural analog to the seed's proposed composite-rotor/AMB design; a credible European competitor not yet in the AI-datacenter niche specifically.

india_origin_audit.notes: Germany-headquartered (Julich, North Rhine-Westphalia).

title: Piller Buys Cash-Strapped Active Power
claim_supported: Piller (Langley Holdings/UK) acquired financially distressed Active Power (Austin, TX) in 2016 specifically for its flywheel-based UPS technology, committing to keep manufacturing in Austin.
locator: Article body, acquisition-rationale paragraphs, dated 2016-10-03
notes: Establishes the incumbent's (Piller's) US manufacturing/flywheel heritage lineage -- context for competitive-entrenchment assessment.

india_origin_audit.notes: US trade press.

title: NVIDIA 800 VDC Architecture Will Power the Next Generation of AI Factories
claim_supported: Full-scale production of 800VDC data centers to coincide with NVIDIA Kyber rack-scale systems in 2027; 576 Rubin Ultra GPUs/rack; targets >1MW/rack (up from ~200kW baseline).
locator: Blog post body, timeline and power-density paragraphs, dated 2025-05-20
notes: Concrete, dated, industry-roadmap 2027 trigger for the DC-native buffering socket the seed depends on; vendor source, treated as roadmap context not independent validation.

india_origin_audit.notes: US public company, HQ Santa Clara, CA.

title: Fourth Power raises $19M to scale new thermal battery technology using liquid tin
claim_supported: Fourth Power raised $45M total ($19M Series A 2023 + ~$20M Series A-Plus 2025); liquid-tin thermal battery targeting $25/kWh; 1MWh demo near Boston; 'letters of expressed interest from three [unnamed] utilities' -- explicitly not a signed order.
locator: Article body, funding and customer-interest paragraphs, dated 2025-12-22
notes: Central finding: no named commercial buyer/order exists yet for the flagship liquid-metal thermal-battery developer -- the seed's own 2028 gate is unsatisfied on current evidence.

india_origin_audit.notes: US trade press; Fourth Power HQ Boston/Bedford, MA area.

title: Turning extreme heat into large-scale energy storage
claim_supported: Fourth Power uses liquid tin (not silicon), pumped through graphite piping at 1,900-2,400C into insulated carbon storage blocks, converted back via TPV cells; founder Asegun Henry (MIT professor) holds a Guinness liquid-metal-pumping record (1,200-1,400C).
locator: Article body, technology-description paragraphs, dated 2026-03-18
notes: Clarifies a technical distinction the seed conflates: Fourth Power (commercial, tin) vs. the separate MIT molten-silicon lab record (atlas L04-108) are related but distinct efforts.

india_origin_audit.notes: US university news office.

title: SCG Cleanergy & Rondo Energy to Deploy World's First Heat Battery to Power Cement Production
claim_supported: Named commercial customer SCG Cleanergy (Siam Cement Group subsidiary) deploying a Rondo firebrick heat battery at a cement plant in Saraburi, Thailand -- solid-media architecture, not liquid metal.
locator: Press release body, customer/deployment paragraphs, dated 2024-05-15
notes: Real, named, dated commercial deployment for the competing solid-media architecture -- shows solid media is commercially ahead of liquid-metal architectures, amplifying the seed's own timing_window_risk.

india_origin_audit.notes: Rondo Energy HQ Hayward/Alameda, CA; SCG Cleanergy is a Thailand-based subsidiary of Siam Cement Group.
